mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in nonfarm payrolls indicates strong labor market demand, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with PAYEMS growth > 100k, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with HOUST declines, or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures and monetary easing, creating a tailwind for equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with concurrent CPI/FEDFUNDS declines, or interaction term coefficient not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production signals stronger corporate output, improving earnings prospects and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with INDPRO growth > 1%, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth exceeds 2% month-over-month, it signals strong consumer spending, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of triggered periods, or threshold crossing fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer confidence and causing S&P 500 negative returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 50%+ of triggered periods, or UNRATE change fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP and housing starts signal a demand collapse, generating S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with GDPC1/HOUST declines, or interaction term coefficient not significant (p > 0.05).
